vault.teaman/ a working garden
dailydecisions
Obsidian

The local-first markdown app this vault lives in. Links are first-class, everything is a plain text file, and the graph is yours to shape.

@zshoham/teaman

The engine that builds this site.

Astro

The web framework underneath.

lucide
changelog
docs

API & plugin reference.

forum
made slowly
filtered by
28 jun 2026slides8 slides

Teaman — Knowledge as a Website

Your Obsidian vault, published automatically as a static site.

28 jun 2026guide598 words

Using This System

This system turns an Obsidian vault into a static website, published automatically on every commit to GitLab.

10 may 2026note84 words

Sunday, may 10

Pruned 26 daily notes from Q1 into 4 evergreens. The ratio feels right — most of what looks important on a Tuesday is gone by Friday.

09 may 2026note405 words

The Fidelity Ladder

Match the fidelity of the artifact to the conversation you want to have. Mismatching them costs the conversation, every time.

09 may 2026note671 words

Vault Static Site Architecture

Design decisions for the Teaman vault's static site pipeline.

09 may 2026note46 words

Saturday, may 9

No screens until 11. Coffee on the step. Started a paper notebook for the second mile — the walking one I keep talking about and never actually carry.

08 may 2026note69 words

Friday, may 8

Weekly retro moved to the café on the corner. The change of room did the work I keep hoping a new template will do. We finished in 38 minutes.

07 may 2026note252 words

Soft Deadlines

A deadline that slips without consequence trains everyone to discount the next one. This is the whole essay, but it is worth drawing out *why* the discount compounds.

07 may 2026note320 words

Tools for Thought, Minus the Manifesto

Most "tools for thought" decks are manifestos in disguise. They sell a worldview and ship a backlog. The honest version is much smaller and much more boring.

07 may 2026note70 words

Thursday, may 7

Edit pass on the cadence guide, on paper. Three things to cut, one section to move. The shape of the piece is finally visible.

06 may 2026note269 words

Shipping Cadence

The cadence is the product. A team that ships every week is a different organism from one that ships every quarter, even building the same thing.

06 may 2026note71 words

Wednesday, may 6

Design review — showed the new daily-notes layout. Two pieces of pushback worth keeping: the week banner needs a date range, and the day glyphs were too loud.

05 may 2026note101 words

Tuesday, may 5

Slept well for the first time in a week. Wrote 600 words on the shipping-cadence guide before opening Slack.

04 may 2026note102 words

Monday, may 4

Started the morning by re-reading Sophie Leroy on attention residue. The cost is not the switch itself — it is the unfinished thought left behind, idling in cache.

03 may 2026note35 words

Sunday, may 3

Worth keeping: the weeks I write the least in here are the ones that go best. The vault is for the thinking I cannot finish in my head, not the thinking I am already doing.

02 may 2026note25 words

Saturday, may 2

No vault, no laptop, no notes app. Just a paper book and a long bike ride. The notes app survived the day without me, somehow.

01 may 2026note41 words

Friday, may 1

Short retro — only three of us in the office. The remote half drove it, which was better than the usual hybrid muddle.

28 apr 2026note46 words

Tuesday, april 28

Re-reading the running-1on1s guide. Two edits: cut the bit about meeting templates, add a paragraph on cancelling sparingly. Templates are a tax; cancellation policy is a signal.

27 apr 2026note59 words

Monday, april 27

Back from the long weekend with a clearer head than I deserve. Wrote out the week as a list of three things, taped it to the monitor.

26 apr 2026note25 words

Sunday, april 26

Reading-week energy is a thing. Probably worth engineering — block a calendar week per quarter and just protect it. Even half a week would help.

24 apr 2026note27 words

Friday, april 24

Weekly retro — closed three loops. Opened one. Acceptable.

23 apr 2026note49 words

Thursday, april 23

Edit on paper. Two essays printed at the small font; red pen; cut nineteen percent by word count without losing anything.

22 apr 2026note29 words

Wednesday, april 22

With T. T. wants help structuring a workshop on feedback. Pulled up the old deck, walked them through the three slides that matter and the fourteen that do not.

21 apr 2026note47 words

Tuesday, april 21

Three things cancelled in a row. The calendar gift. Sat down and finished the year-in-margins deck — 34 slides, organised by question, not by genre.

20 apr 2026note44 words

Monday, april 20

Monday opening: three calls, two of which could have been a paragraph. Wrote the paragraphs anyway, sent them as a pre-read, watched the calls compress.

19 apr 2026note23 words

Sunday, april 19

Reset day. Cleared the inbox to under fifty, archived the trip notes, set up Monday with three things on it and nothing more.

17 apr 2026note23 words

Friday, april 17

Back home. Made the cardinal mistake of opening Slack before unpacking, and lost two hours to threads that would have resolved themselves overnight.

15 apr 2026note44 words

Wednesday, april 15

Long breakfast with the local team. The interesting questions arrived only after the coffee was finished — worth budgeting more time on day-one of any visit.

13 apr 2026note28 words

Monday, april 13

Landed in Lisbon at six in the morning, slept in the cab, and made the first meeting by ten. Felt like a stunt. The day did not recover.

12 apr 2026note22 words

Sunday, april 12

Sunday reading: a paper on slack in complex systems. The thesis maps onto teams cleanly — productivity peaks well before utilisation does.

10 apr 2026note42 words

Friday, april 10

Decisions made in groups feel slower than they are. The hour we just spent saved a fortnight of back-and-forth in DMs.

08 apr 2026note25 words

Wednesday, april 8

Quarterly planning. Three hours, six people, one document. The document did most of the work. The room corrected two errors I would have shipped otherwise.

29 mar 2026note19 words

Sunday, march 29

Long ride. Came back with three solutions to a problem I had not yet articulated, which is the trick.

26 mar 2026note50 words

Thursday, march 26

Pair-paged with K. on the daily layout. We disagreed about the spine; both versions ended up on paper, side by side.

24 mar 2026note52 words

Tuesday, march 24

Sketched the new daily-notes view on the back of a delivery receipt. The good idea, in retrospect: week as the unit, day as the section, no per-entry chrome.

15 mar 2026note21 words

Sunday, march 15

The post-bad-week ritual: a long walk, a slow lunch, no opening of any work tab until Monday morning. Earned, not negotiated.

13 mar 2026note24 words

Friday, march 13

Retro · whole team. Bad weeks make better retros. We came out of it with two changes that should have been made months ago.

12 mar 2026note29 words

Thursday, march 12

Long evening on the incident. Closed the loop at half past nine, ate cold takeaway, and made the rookie mistake of replying to a Slack thread instead of sleeping.

22 feb 2026note42 words

Sunday, february 22

Finished the outline for the year-in-margins deck. Six questions, forty books, a way of arranging them that does not feel like a list.

05 feb 2026decision89 words

ADR-0018 · Migrate to active-active multi-region

Serve writes from multiple regions concurrently to use standby capacity and cut failover time.

11 oct 2025decision78 words

ADR-0017 · Edge caching for read-heavy APIs

Cache cacheable read responses at the edge with explicit invalidation on writes.

20 jul 2025decision88 words

ADR-0016 · Replace REST webhooks with an event bus

Offer a durable event bus for integrators instead of best-effort outbound webhooks.

03 apr 2025decision96 words

ADR-0015 · Envelope encryption for secrets at rest

Encrypt each secret with a per-record data key wrapped by a KMS-managed master key.

15 jan 2025decision85 words

ADR-0014 · Cell-based architecture for tenant isolation

Partition tenants into independent cells so a failure or noisy neighbour is contained.

02 sep 2024decision75 words

ADR-0013 · OpenTelemetry for tracing and metrics

Standardise instrumentation on OpenTelemetry to stay vendor-neutral across backends.

14 jul 2024decision77 words

ADR-0012 · Server-side sessions in Redis

Replace client-held JWTs with opaque session tokens backed by a Redis store.

30 may 2024decision82 words

ADR-0011 · GraphQL gateway for client APIs

Put a GraphQL gateway in front of internal services for the web and mobile clients.

11 mar 2024decision77 words

ADR-0010 · Move background work to a managed queue

Offload long-running and retryable work to a managed message queue with dead-letter handling.

22 jan 2024decision83 words

ADR-0009 · Multi-region active-passive deployment

Run a hot standby region with asynchronous replication and a documented failover.

19 oct 2023decision83 words

ADR-0008 · Centralised feature-flag service

Gate risky changes behind flags evaluated by one service, decoupling deploy from release.

01 aug 2023decision71 words

ADR-0007 · Kubernetes for orchestration

Run services on managed Kubernetes rather than hand-rolled instances or a PaaS.

12 jun 2023decision88 words

ADR-0006 · Event sourcing for the audit log

Model the security audit trail as an append-only event stream, projected into read models.

08 apr 2023decision89 words

ADR-0005 · Stateless JWT sessions stored client-side

Issue signed JWTs held by the client so the API can stay stateless.

15 mar 2023decision92 words

ADR-0004 · REST over GraphQL for the public API

Ship a resource-oriented REST API first; revisit GraphQL once client needs are clearer.

20 feb 2023decision99 words

ADR-0003 · PostgreSQL as the primary datastore

Standardise on PostgreSQL for transactional data before reaching for anything specialised.

02 feb 2023decision93 words

ADR-0002 · Use a single monorepo

Keep all services and shared libraries in one repository with a unified build graph.

10 jan 2023decision121 words

ADR-0001 · Record architecture decisions

Adopt lightweight ADRs (Nygard format) checked into the repo, one Markdown file per decision.

nothing matches.

topics

57 entries · last edit 28 jun 2026made slowly
